fact: Mission Control Phase 3 stack completed (Apr 6): Phase 3A autonomous Sudo decision modes (direct_answer/ask_question/propose_alternative/delegate), Phase 3B bounded lane sequencing + synthesis, Phase 3C Marvin oversight/escalation/approval boundaries. Full stack live for real workflow testing.

First successful real Sudo implementation run (Apr 6): Sudo orchestration + FE lane delegation produced real sidebar redesign, committed as nested repo abbfb1b7. Proof that Dev Team/Sudo workflow can take a scoped implementation plan and land a good result.

Three Sudo plumbing bugs fixed during seat integration (Apr 6): (1) Sudo seat composer submits routed to orchestration not Marvin path, (2) model selection moved from chat-based /model to session setup phase, (3) JSON parser now uses schema-aware scoring to prefer Sudo decision payload over wrapper.

Skills preferences are now server-backed: /api/skills/preferences route + skills-ui-state.json. localStorage is migration/fallback only, not the primary store. Read more/Close summary on long skill descriptions now uses contextual pop cards.

Weather widget redesign plan created (Apr 6): floating orb design concept, 100px glassmorphic circle, time-of-day colour palette with 7 slots and 2-second CSS transitions, subtle pulsing glow. Plan artifact: projects/mission-control/docs/weather-widget-implementation-plan.md.

STT Phase 1 completed with local Whisper port (Apr 7, 2026): Mission Control speech-to-text now supports local self-hosted transcription via @fugood/whisper.node. lib/transcribe.ts supports both openai and local providers. Preview runtime switched to local provider, base.en model downloaded to /data/.nerve/models. Voice diagnostics improved to distinguish secure-context failure, missing getUserMedia, and missing MediaRecorder (with Safari/private-mode hinting).

Sudo completion normalization (Apr 7, 2026): Sudo panel now selects newest orchestration by recency (updatedAt/completedAt/requestedAt) instead of preferring older active waiting runs. Older unresolved clarification runs no longer overshadow completed implementation runs in the Sudo panel.

Standalone specialist seats implementation plan created (Apr 7, 2026): Philippe wants standalone specialists as real direct-chat seats in the seat dropdown, using existing agent workspaces/skills rather than Marvin impersonation. Japin called out as benchmark for specialist-scoped memory/learning continuity. Plan artifact: projects/mission-control/docs/standalone-specialist-seats-implementation-plan.md.

Mission Control web research migrated to SearXNG (Apr 7, 2026): Preview runtime now uses SearXNG at http://72.60.232.55:32768 instead of DuckDuckGo HTML. Philippe prefers SearXNG quality and already runs the service on the VPS. Provider support added to scripts/lib/web-research.mjs and lib/autonomous-preflight.ts.

Autonomous task execution hardening (Apr 7, 2026): Planning-only tasks now carry explicit no-implementation instruction and reject non-markdown execution artifacts. MiniMax task runs now start with thinking=high from the initial execution call rather than inheriting old priority-based default.

Mission Control Chat top-section wrap completed (Apr 7, 2026): Sudo orchestration panel made scrollable, redundant Sudo decisions heading removed, Chat page chrome tightened. Completed Sudo runs now surface summary-first with artifact-aware deliverable block.

Memory module refinement pass applied: moved Memory lower in sidebar, removed Path field from metadata strip, removed unnecessary subtitles, changed active-state styling to higher-contrast teal, added Back to top button.

Search product architecture confirmed: Memory = browse/read memory + search inside memory files, Files = browse + preview + later embedded file search for non-memory content, standalone Search page = temporary bridge only to be retired after embedded file search exists in Files.
Product boundary confirmed: Files must remain non-memory; memory search belongs in Memory, not in Files.
Product posture confirmed: Mission Control remains preview/build shell until Chat path is production-usable; existing Gateway Chat UI remains the real working chat surface for now.

Chat readiness rule: do not treat Mission Control as default daily-driver chat until embedded/live chat path is reliably reachable, honest about auth/boundary conditions, and genuinely improves the experience.
